condition_precedents: Demand-based scheduling system (e.g. Kronos, NICE WFM) deployed
Labour standards (tasks per hour) established and validated
Store manager training on scheduling optimisation completed
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — store labour productivity benchmarks (sales per FTE)
McKinsey & Company — Reimagining the Store (mckinsey.com/industries/retail) — labour scheduling optimisation 8-18%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct time-and-motion study at representative stores: measure labour hours by department (cashier, replenishment, customer service) vs transaction volume
Implement workforce management software with footfall-linked demand forecasting and automated shift generation by store
Convert fixed-schedule staff to flexible/part-time model in high-footfall-variance stores; negotiate new employment terms with union where applicable
Track labour cost as % of store revenue, transactions per labour hour, and overtime % monthly by store format

diagnostic_signals.signal: Labour cost as % of store revenue >12% vs organised retail benchmark of <9% for similar format
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand-linked scheduling will reduce excess labour cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Labour hours in low-footfall hours (weekday mornings, late evenings) scheduled at same staffing as peak hours
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Footfall-linked scheduling will reduce wasted labour hours

diagnostic_signals.signal: Overtime as % of total labour cost >10% consistently, indicating demand forecasting gap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improved scheduling will reduce overtime premium

required_data_fields: Hourly footfall data by store and day type (weekday, weekend, festival)
Labour hours by department and shift pattern
Labour cost per store (permanent, part-time, casual)
Transactions per hour by department and store format
Union contract terms and flexibility provisions

condition_precedents: Category profitability analysis completed (GMROI per category)
Joint business planning (JBP) framework established with top 20 vendors
Vendor trade funding terms benchmarked and renegotiated
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — vendor funding and category management benchmarks India
Nielsen — Retail Execution Excellence 2024 (nielsen.com/insights/) — category management ROI
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build category P&L for top-20 categories: map gross margin, shrinkage, markdown, and space cost; rank by profit per sq ft
Conduct planogram audit for top-10 revenue categories: identify space allocation misalignment vs sales velocity and margin contribution
Negotiate category captain or co-management agreements with top-3 suppliers per category for planogram and ranging support
Implement ranging decisions and planogram changes; track sales per sq ft, in-stock rate, and category margin quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Profit per sq ft below company average for ≥30% of category space allocation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Category management and space reallocation will improve profit density

diagnostic_signals.signal: Supplier co-investment (in-store activation, planogram compliance) <50% of category leader peer benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Category captain engagement will increase supplier investment and reduce category cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Out-of-stock rate >5% for top-50 SKUs in high-velocity categories
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improved planogram and range clarity will reduce lost sales from stockouts

required_data_fields: Category P&L by category (gross margin, shrinkage, markdown, space cost)
Space allocation (sq ft) vs sales per sq ft by category
Supplier co-investment and trade terms by category
In-stock (OSA) data by category and SKU
Planogram compliance monitoring data

condition_precedents: Shrinkage audit completed by store / category / shift
AI-powered CCTV or EAS (electronic article surveillance) deployed
Loss prevention team KPIs linked to shrinkage targets
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — shrinkage rates for Indian organised retail
FICCI — India Retail Summit Report 2024 (ficci.in) — loss prevention and shrinkage benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Decompose total shrinkage by root cause: known theft (internal/external), unknown loss, and administrative errors; estimate % contribution by store
Deploy EAS (Electronic Article Surveillance) and RFID for high-shrink categories; install AI-powered CCTV at self-checkout and high-risk zones
Implement exception-based reporting for POS anomalies (voids, refunds, no-sales) to surface internal theft patterns
Track shrinkage % by store and category monthly; benchmark against FICCI/retail association benchmark; reward LP performance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Shrinkage >1.5% of net sales vs organised retail benchmark of <1.0%, indicating structural loss prevention gap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: LP investment will yield positive ROI through shrinkage reduction

diagnostic_signals.signal: Unknown loss (book to physical gap) >70% of total shrinkage with no root-cause visibility
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RFID and cycle counting will improve visibility and enable targeted intervention

diagnostic_signals.signal: High-risk categories (electronics, cosmetics, apparel) contributing >50% of shrinkage without dedicated LP coverage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: EAS and enhanced surveillance for high-risk categories will reduce loss

required_data_fields: Shrinkage data by store, category, and loss cause (known theft, unknown, admin error)
Current loss prevention technology by store format
POS exception data (voids, refunds, no-sales by cashier)
RFID and EAS coverage map by store
Historical LP investment vs shrinkage reduction correlation

condition_precedents: Consumer acceptance testing completed for own-brand alternatives
Contract manufacturing partner qualified with quality audit
Shelf placement strategy agreed with commercial team
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — private label penetration and margin premium benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map category white space: identify segments where retailer private label penetration <10% and competitor PL or NBD is gaining share
Qualify manufacturers for PL production: supplier audit, GMP/FSSAI compliance, formulation capability, and exclusivity terms
Launch PL range in pilot stores (>20 stores) with consumer testing; track consumer acceptance vs branded alternative
Scale to full chain; track PL penetration, category margin uplift, and branded manufacturer response

diagnostic_signals.signal: Category gross margin <25% with retailer private label equivalent achievable at >35% margin on same shelf
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Private label will structurally improve category margin mix

diagnostic_signals.signal: Private label penetration in category <15% vs modern trade benchmark of >25% in comparable markets
diagnostic_signals.confirms: PL expansion headroom is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Consumer willingness to trade down confirmed by market research for this category
diagnostic_signals.confirms: PL launch risk is manageable

required_data_fields: Category margin data by brand, pack size, and store format
Private label penetration by category and store format
Consumer research on PL acceptance by category
Manufacturer shortlist with production capacity and compliance data
Competitor PL pricing and ranging data

condition_precedents: Order management system (OMS) upgraded to support multi-node fulfilment
Click-and-collect and dark store economics validated
Last-mile cost per delivery benchmarked and optimised
industry_sources: CRISIL — India E-Commerce and Logistics Report 2024 (crisil.com) — last-mile delivery cost benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map current fulfilment flow for online orders: DC-to-home, store-to-home, and click-and-collect; measure cost per order and SLA by fulfilment path
Model dark store feasibility for top-5 urban clusters by order density; build order routing algorithm to minimise cost per order by zone
Deploy OMS with real-time inventory visibility across DC and store; automate order routing based on proximity, stock, and SLA
Track cost per order by fulfilment path, on-time delivery rate, and returns rate monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Online order fulfilment cost >18% of order value vs benchmark <12% for comparable basket size and geography
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Fulfilment optimisation (dark store, order routing) will reduce unit fulfilment cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: DC-to-home fulfilment contributing >60% of orders in high-density urban areas where store-based fulfilment is cheaper
diagnostic_signals.confirms: OMS-driven smart routing will shift volume to lower-cost local fulfilment

diagnostic_signals.signal: Returns rate >12% of orders due to late delivery or wrong-item fulfilment issues
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Fulfilment accuracy improvement will reduce reverse logistics cost

required_data_fields: Online order data by fulfilment path, cost, and SLA performance
Store and DC inventory position (real-time) by SKU
Customer geography and order density by pin code
OMS capability assessment for real-time routing
Dark store operational model and capex/opex estimate

condition_precedents: Demand forecasting integrated with markdown trigger rules
AI-driven markdown recommendation tool piloted on key categories
Buying team trained on inventory-optimised ordering
industry_sources: Nielsen — Retail Inventory Management 2024 (nielsen.com/insights/) — markdown optimisation and clearance benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse markdown pattern by category: measure markdown rate (% of sales at discount), depth (average discount %), and clearance velocity
Build AI-driven markdown optimisation model: predict optimal timing and depth of markdown to maximise margin × volume vs clearance deadline
Pilot model in 2 high-markdown categories (fashion, seasonal); measure margin improvement vs historical average; refine before scale
Roll out to all seasonal and fashion categories; track markdown as % of sales and gross margin improvement monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Markdown as % of gross sales >8% for seasonal categories with unpredictable demand (fashion, festive, seasonal food)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI-driven markdown optimisation will reduce excess clearance discount

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average markdown depth increasing YoY (clearance discount deepening) despite similar sell-through rate
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Earlier and more targeted markdown will improve margin per unit cleared

diagnostic_signals.signal: End-of-season inventory >15% of season-opening stock requiring clearance at >50% discount
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand planning and markdown model improvement will reduce clearance overhang

required_data_fields: SKU-level markdown data: timing, depth, and volume cleared by discount band
Season-end inventory and clearance sell-through rate
Demand forecast accuracy for seasonal categories
Category margin (with and without markdown) by season
Clearance deadline requirements by category

condition_precedents: Points liability and redemption cost modelled by member cohort
Programme mechanics redesigned for lower cost-per-engagement
Incremental revenue attributable to loyalty validated by finance
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — loyalty programme cost as % of sales and redemption benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit loyalty program economics: measure member acquisition cost, active member rate, redemption rate, and incremental spend uplift vs non-member baseline
Rationalise point structure: model break-even point earn/burn ratio; eliminate no-value redemption categories; simplify tier structure
Improve personalisation: use member purchase data for targeted offers; shift from mass discount to 1:1 relevant reward to improve redemption ROI
Track loyalty breakage rate, active member %, incremental basket value, and loyalty cost as % of revenue monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Loyalty program cost (points liability + admin) >3% of revenue with incremental basket uplift <2% vs non-member
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Program rationalisation and personalisation will improve ROI on loyalty investment

diagnostic_signals.signal: Active member rate <30% of enrolled members, indicating poor engagement and wasted enrolment cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improving personalisation and offer relevance will increase active member rate

diagnostic_signals.signal: Breakage rate <10% indicating high redemption liability relative to margin benefit
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Adjusting earn/burn ratio will reduce points liability without impacting retention

required_data_fields: Loyalty program P&L: enrolment cost, points issued, redemption cost, and program administration
Active member rate and purchase frequency vs non-member
Points earn/burn ratio and breakage rate
Member segmentation by spend tier and redemption behaviour
Basket size uplift data: member vs non-member by category

condition_precedents: Category where direct sourcing is feasible (non-complex, non-regulated goods)
Supplier qualification completed (factory audit, quality certification)
Import / customs expertise available internally or contracted
industry_sources: FICCI — India Retail Supply Chain Report 2024 (ficci.in) — direct sourcing penetration and margin improvement
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map supply chain for top-5 fresh/produce categories: identify intermediaries (commission agents, wholesalers) and their margin take per unit
Identify and qualify direct farm/manufacturer suppliers: assess volume capability, quality standards, and logistic connectivity to DC
Pilot direct procurement for 1–2 categories with contract farming agreements or direct manufacturer supply; track quality and cost vs current supply chain
Scale to all shortlisted categories; track cost per unit (farm gate vs current landed cost), quality compliance, and fill rate monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Intermediary margin >25% of landed cost for categories where direct sourcing is physically and quality-feasibly achievable
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Direct sourcing will eliminate intermediary margin and improve price competitiveness

diagnostic_signals.signal: Product quality or freshness complaints from customers >5% of category revenue, indicating supply chain length impact on quality
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Shorter supply chain will improve freshness and reduce quality-related returns

diagnostic_signals.signal: Own-label or private-label strategy requiring assured supply that is not available through spot market
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Direct supply agreement will secure volume commitment and quality consistency

required_data_fields: Current supply chain map: intermediary count, margin by tier, and landed cost by category
Supplier qualification criteria and MOQ for direct sourcing
Quality specifications and cold chain requirements by category
DC logistics connectivity to potential direct sourcing locations
Contract farming regulatory requirements and templates

condition_precedents: Store-level P&L and footfall data available for at least 24 months
Catchment area and trade-zone analysis completed
Lease break or non-renewal strategy agreed for underperforming stores
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — retail store productivity and breakeven benchmarks India
JLL India — India Retail Real Estate Report 2024 (jll.com/en-in/research) — rental benchmarks and store network optimisation
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build store-level unit economics: revenue per sq ft, four-wall EBITDA, and payback period by store age, format, and location tier
Conduct catchment analysis for under-performing stores: assess cannibalisation, population density, and competitive intensity to distinguish structural vs temporary underperformance
For close/exit candidates: negotiate lease exit or surrender at break clause; for under-performing stores: implement format change or rightsizing
Track four-wall EBITDA margin, revenue per sq ft, and lease-adjusted payback by store monthly; gate new store investments against updated return hurdles

diagnostic_signals.signal: ≥15% of stores with negative four-wall EBITDA for ≥2 consecutive years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Exit or format change for loss-making stores will remove P&L drag

diagnostic_signals.signal: New store payback period >4 years for recent openings vs investment hurdle of ≤3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Network strategy review will tighten store quality and return on capital

diagnostic_signals.signal: Lease WALE >3 years in locations where store has not reached breakeven, creating exit cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Proactive negotiation at lease break will reduce exit cost

required_data_fields: Store P&L: revenue, four-wall EBITDA, and payback period by store
Catchment analysis data: population, demographics, and competitive landscape by store
Lease terms: WALE, break-clause dates, and exit cost by store
Store age and format classification
New store investment return hurdles and pipeline

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
